Uh I always said that there's four things that make our product really unique.
Uh The first thing being is that we're fully automated, so not having to go out there and manually collect the data yourselves.
Uh The second thing is that we actually update our data every single day.

So for the sites that are out there on those rev management tools, uh you know, changing rates regularly, our goal is for you to be able to jump into our platform and see exactly what's going in the marketplace today.
Um What else is great is that you can actually track any property as long as they have a website and use a property management software, they do not have to be our customers whatsoever.
So we can really track the entire market, which is great.
And then also again, II I loop this in because it's, it's relevant now, but we are 100% publicly available data.
There's zero shared information here, nothing to do with anything in regards to that real uh real page and now you lawsuit.
So all publicly available data.

Do you have a team of analysts or on site?
Team members, I guess.

three.

So we're not necessarily using them to set our rates.
Uh We like to know where we are, but in several of the markets specifically in Urbana, Illinois, Decatur, Illinois, um even uh in Indianapolis, our rents are higher than our competitors.
So we're not, we're not really looking at it from that standpoint.

Although we, we gather that information, we're also looking at occupancies, uh mostly we're using those rates so that when someone comes into our leasing center and they've seen our property, you know, before, uh they leave one of the questions the agent is asking is, you know, where, where else are you looking?
And if it's one of our competitors, we already have their rates, we have their floor plans, we show them, uh they're in the office before they leave, how we compare to them.
Uh Even though our rents might be higher, the amenities that we offer that they don't.
Uh and what makes us a better choice?
Yeah.
So hopefully, you know, they just, they just lease there that day without even going to look at the competitors.
But we want to know our competitors and then we incentivize our property managers to get to know the managers at those other sites.
So they meet with them um, quarterly, they'll take them to lunch or they'll take them a gift card and go, you know, as a thank you for helping us compete complete our survey.
So we generally will get more information than what maybe their corporate office wants them to give.

Um And it, we generally consider it more accurate than what they might be giving someone else because there's a report there that they might not have with another person that's just randomly calling them.
Yeah, that makes sense.

Let me kind of show you what we have.

So you know your markets really well, you say, hey, these are the, you know, X amount of comps you want to track.
Great, we can build it out of that way.

Um And then lastly, what's great is you can always add or remove a property to your comp at any time you'd like.
So you're, you're completely in control of your comp set, which is, which is nice.

I think that there's some great usage in here or great examples in here for every department of the organization.

Uh Once you're in here, the very top, this will be your main navigation.

I think very representative of your standard market survey.

So the number of leases over the last 37 and the number of leases each property does average per week.
Um A big one too is we will let you know if there's a rent special or concession tied to the property or tied to a specific unit in the property.
Um So nice to have that context, you know, is the competition offering to, to waive admin or a fees or are they looking to, you know, offer a month or two months free rent?

So nice from that perspective, um We will also calculate net effective rent and that effective price per foot for you at the property level, at the floor plan level and all the way down to the unit level.
Um The second section of this would be pricing and availability.
So this is broken out by floor plan.
Um You know, the idea here is to essentially understand, you know, where our one bedroom position compared to the competition.

So we'll show you the gross rent again.

We'll let you know if there's a concession attached to this floor plan, we'll show you net effective rent, square footage price per foot, net effective price per foot and the number of one bedrooms each property does have have available in it right now.
So obviously you mentioned you want to be, you know, the most expensive one bedrooms in the comp set right now.

And now it's time to actually lower rent prices to get a little bit more competitive.
So just an easy way to visualize that.
